core_payload.content:
# Symphony Run Attempt

## 1. Role
Symphony Run Attempt is the durable assignment-bearing execution record for repository work, packaging the live session, its artifacts, and its branch consequences into one accountable object.

## 2. Attempt Contract
Attempt records carry clearer assignment handles, return paths, artifact bundles, outcome linkage, and a stronger distinction between the durable attempt object and the live session nested inside it.

## 3. Operational Consequence
Repository work remains reviewable and attributable even as it becomes more asynchronous, more persistent, and more tightly coupled to downstream review and promotion lanes.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Symphony Run Attempt supports To Dig Deep by making each execution pass durable enough to inspect, compare, and govern.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Run Attempt defines the durable execution record for repository work inside N1Hub. It packages assignment handles, artifact bundles, branch consequences, status transitions, and downstream review linkage into one accountable object that remains distinct from the live session nested inside it. The capsule matters because asynchronous repository work needs a persistent record that can be inspected, compared, retried, and governed after the live conversation has moved on. It turns execution from a transient event into a durable operational artifact with clear ownership and lifecycle boundaries.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so Symphony Run Attempt states execution doctrine directly.
